english: Hey, are you going to the game today?
choicesEnglish: Hey, are you going to the game today?
Hey, are you going to the movies tonight?
Hey, are you going to the museum now?
Hey, are you going to the port later?
correctAnswerEnglish: Hey, are you going to the game today?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Una domanda comune tra amici amanti dello sport.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo se l'amico va allo stadio per la sfida.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A common question among sports-loving friends.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking if the friend is going to the stadium for the match.

choicesEnglish: Which city are you leaving for?
Which bar are you paying for?
Which trip are you running for?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! La domanda fondamentale per conoscere la passione sportiva.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi sapere per chi batte il cuore dell'amico.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The fundamental question to know someone's sports passion.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to know who your friend supports.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Dichiarare la propria appartenenza a una squadra.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai dicendo di chi sei sostenitore.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Declaring your allegiance to a team.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're saying who you are a supporter of.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Specificare l'avversario della giornata.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indichi chi è lo sfidante della tua squadra.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Specifying the day's opponent.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're indicating who the challenger for your team is.

english: The stadium is huge, isn't it?
choicesEnglish: The stadium is huge, isn't it?
The bar is very dirty, isn't it?
The tour is very boring, isn't it?
The bill is very expensive, isn't it?
correctAnswerEnglish: The stadium is huge, isn't it?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Commentare le dimensioni imponenti dell'impianto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai notando quanto sia vasto lo stadio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Commenting on the impressive size of the facility.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're noticing how vast the stadium is.

italian: Ho un posto numerato in tribuna.
english: I have a numbered seat in the stands.
choicesItalian: Ho un posto numerato in tribuna.
Ho un bacio caro in borsa.
Ho un regalo finto in via.
Ho un debito alto in bar.
choicesEnglish: I have a numbered seat in the stands.
I have an expensive kiss in the bag.
I have a fake gift in the street.
I have a high debt in the bar.
correctAnswerItalian: Ho un posto numerato in tribuna.
correctAnswerEnglish: I have a numbered seat in the stands.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Specificare il tipo e la zona del sedile.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, parli della tua posizione assegnata allo stadio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Specifying the seat type and area.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are talking about your assigned position at the stadium.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Chiedere la zona specifica degli spalti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi sapere in quale parte dello stadio si trova il posto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Asking for the specific area of the stands.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to know which part of the stadium the seat is in.

choicesEnglish: There is a lot of boredom.
There is a lot of dirt.
There is a lot of haste.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Notare il grande affollamento per la partita.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica che lo stadio è quasi pieno.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Noticing the large crowd for the match.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates that the stadium is almost full.

english: Go Roma! Come on, like that!
choicesEnglish: Go Roma! Come on, like that!
correctAnswerEnglish: Go Roma! Come on, like that!

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Un incitamento classico del tifoso giallorosso.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai gridando per sostenere la tua squadra.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A classic chant from a Roma fan.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are shouting to support your team.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Apprezzare lo sviluppo del gioco sul campo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, esprimi entusiasmo per una fase pericolosa della partita.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Appreciating the development of the game on the field.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you express enthusiasm for a dangerous phase of the match.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Lamentarsi di un ostacolo visivo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, hai la vista coperta da qualcuno o qualcosa.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Complaining about a visual obstacle.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, your view is blocked by someone or something.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Chiedere a qualcuno di non stare in piedi.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è un invito cortese a prendere posto sul sedile.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Asking someone not to stand up.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's a polite invitation to take a seat.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Esclamazione di gioia per un punto segnato.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, celebri il momento in cui la palla va in rete.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Exclamation of joy for a point scored.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you celebrate the moment when the ball goes into the net.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Chiedere se il gioco è stato fermato.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi sapere se c'è stato il segnale acustico ufficiale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Asking if the game was stopped.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to know if there was the official acoustic signal.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Confermare che il goal è regolare per l'arbitro.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica che la segnatura è valida.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Confirming that the goal is regular for the referee.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates that the score is valid.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Suggerire di controllare il punteggio sullo schermo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai indicando il display dei risultati.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Suggesting to check the score on the screen.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're pointing to the results display.

italian: C'è molta tensione tra i tifosi.
english: There's a lot of tension among the fans.
choicesItalian: C'è molta tensione tra i tifosi.
C'è molta gioia tra i vicini.
C'è molta noia tra i re.
C'è molta pace tra i lupi.
choicesEnglish: There's a lot of tension among the fans.
There's a lot of joy among the neighbors.
There's a lot of boredom among the kings.
There's a lot of peace among the wolves.
correctAnswerItalian: C'è molta tensione tra i tifosi.
correctAnswerEnglish: There's a lot of tension among the fans.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Descrivere l'atmosfera nervosa dello stadio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, parla del clima agitato tra i sostenitori.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Describing the nervous atmosphere of the stadium.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it talks about the agitated climate among supporters.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Un consiglio di prudenza per evitare litigi.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, suggerisci di non agitarsi troppo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A cautionary tip to avoid arguments.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you suggest not getting too worked up.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Per rivendicare la propria posizione numerata.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai dicendo che quel sedile spetta a te.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! To claim your numbered position.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are saying that that seat belongs to you.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Le coordinate precise del tuo biglietto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai leggendo i numeri della tua posizione.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The precise coordinates of your ticket.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are reading the numbers of your position.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Ipotizzare uno sbaglio nella stampa o nell'assegnazione.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, pensi che ci sia qualcosa di sbagliato.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Hypothesizing a mistake in printing or assignment.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you think something is wrong.

choicesEnglish: I lost it on the street.
I saw it at the bar.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Informare sulla modalità di acquisto digitale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indichi che hai preso il biglietto via web.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Informing about the digital purchase method.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you indicate you got the ticket via web.

english: What a mess! / What a noise!
choicesEnglish: What a mess! / What a noise!
correctAnswerEnglish: What a mess! / What a noise!

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un'espressione informale per indicare confusione o rumore.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, ti lamenti della situazione caotica.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! An informal expression for confusion or noise.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're complaining about the chaotic situation.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Cercare l'aiuto del personale dello stadio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi contattare qualcuno che lavora lì per risolvere il problema.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Seeking help from stadium staff.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to contact someone working there to solve the problem.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Chiedere spiegazioni su una situazione insolita.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi capire cosa stia accadendo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Asking for explanations on an unusual situation.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to understand what's going on.

english: You must go to sector C.
choicesEnglish: You must go to sector C.
You must run to bar B.
You must exit at street A.
You must sleep at party D.
correctAnswerEnglish: You must go to sector C.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! L'indicazione corretta data dallo steward.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, ti stanno indirizzando verso la zona giusta.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The correct indication given by the steward.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, they are directing you to the right area.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Commentare i primi 45 minuti di gioco.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, esprimi un parere sulla fase iniziale della partita.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Commenting on the first 45 minutes of the game.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're expressing an opinion on the initial phase of the match.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Un'offesa ironica e comune verso un arbitro che non vede i falli.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, ti stai lamentando degli errori del direttore di gara.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! An ironic and common insult toward a referee who doesn't see fouls.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're complaining about the match director's errors.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Lodare l'intervento del portiere.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai celebrando un salvataggio eccezionale sulla linea.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Praising the goalkeeper's intervention.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're celebrating an exceptional save on the line.

italian: Una birra media e un panino.
english: A medium beer and a sandwich.
choicesItalian: Una birra media e un panino.
Un vino rosso e una pizza.
Un caffè lungo e un bis.
choicesEnglish: A medium beer and a sandwich.
A red wine and a pizza.
A cold water and a sweet.
A long coffee and an encore.
correctAnswerItalian: Una birra media e un panino.
correctAnswerEnglish: A medium beer and a sandwich.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Un'ordinazione classica al bar dello stadio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo da bere e da mangiare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A classic order at the stadium bar.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking for something to drink and eat.

english: There's too much of a line at the bar.
choicesEnglish: There's too much of a line at the bar.
There's too much joy in the street.
There's too much peace in the port.
There's too much boredom at the stadium.
correctAnswerEnglish: There's too much of a line at the bar.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Lamentarsi dell'attesa per essere serviti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indichi che ci sono troppe persone davanti a te.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Complaining about the wait to be served.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're indicating that there are too many people ahead of you.

italian: Sta per iniziare il secondo tempo.
english: The second half is about to start.
choicesItalian: Sta per iniziare il secondo tempo.
Sta per finire la gita lunga.
Sta per correre la borsa nera.
Sta per ridere la festa grande.
choicesEnglish: The second half is about to start.
The long trip is about to end.
The black bag is about to run.
The big party is about to laugh.
correctAnswerItalian: Sta per iniziare il secondo tempo.
correctAnswerEnglish: The second half is about to start.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Bisogna tornare subito sugli spalti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, mancano pochi istanti alla ripresa del gioco.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! You need to get back to the stands right away.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, only moments are left until play resumes.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Il desiderio di ogni tifoso durante la partita.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, auspichi altri goal per la tua squadra.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Every fan's wish during the game.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you hope for more goals for your team.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Chiedere come è finita la partita.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi sapere il punteggio conclusivo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Asking how the match ended.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to know the final score.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! L'esultanza per la vittoria.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, festeggi il successo del tuo club.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The exultation over the victory.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're celebrating your club's success.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Sottolineare il valore dei punti guadagnati.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si parla della posizione della squadra in campionato.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Emphasizing the value of points earned.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's about the team's position in the championship.

english: There's a lot of tension at the exit.
choicesEnglish: There's a lot of tension at the exit.
There's a lot of peace at the entrance.
There's a lot of boredom at the bar.
There's a lot of joy in the square.
correctAnswerEnglish: There's a lot of tension at the exit.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Un avvertimento sul clima tra le opposte tifoserie.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, parla del nervosismo mentre si lascia lo stadio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A warning about the climate between opposing fans.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's about nervousness while leaving the stadium.

english: The bus to get back downtown.
choicesItalian: Il treno per andare in porto.
La gita per correre in via.
La borsa per perdere in bar.
choicesEnglish: The bus to get back downtown.
The train to go to the port.
The trip to run in the street.
The bag to lose in the bar.
correctAnswerEnglish: The bus to get back downtown.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Cercare il mezzo pubblico per rientrare.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai individuando il trasporto per il ritorno.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Looking for public transport to return.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're identifying the return transport.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Un'offerta amichevole per tornare a casa in auto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, ti stai proponendo di accompagnare l'amico col tuo veicolo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A friendly offer to get home by car.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are offering to take your friend in your vehicle.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Per avviare una conversazione sull'azione più bella.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi conferma della segnatura appena avvenuta.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! To start a conversation about the best play.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking for confirmation of the goal that just happened.

choicesEnglish: Break from on top of the roof.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Descrivere la distanza della conclusione verso la porta.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si parla di un goal fatto da lontano.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Describing the distance of the shot toward the goal.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's about a goal scored from afar.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Specificare la modalità del goal.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica che il punto è stato segnato dal dischetto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Specifying the goal's method.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates that the point was scored from the penalty spot.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Esprimere un sostegno moderato verso una squadra.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indichi che ti piace la squadra senza essere un tifoso sfegatato.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Expressing moderate support for a team.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you indicate that you like the team without being a die-hard fan.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Tutti i biglietti erano stati venduti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, parla del grande successo di pubblico per l'evento.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! All tickets had been sold.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it speaks of the great public success of the event.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Un accordo per andare alla prossima partita in gruppo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, suggerisci di acquistare gli ingressi nello stesso momento.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! An agreement to go to the next game as a group.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you suggest buying the entries at the same time.

english: I'll buy the ticket for you.
choicesEnglish: I'll buy the ticket for you.
I'll call the guide for you.
I'll shout the note for you.
correctAnswerEnglish: I'll buy the ticket for you.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Un gesto generoso per invitare l'amico allo stadio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, ti offri di pagare l'ingresso per l'altra persona.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A generous gesture to invite the friend to the stadium.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you offer to pay for the entrance for the other person.

english: There's too much of a crowd at the entrance.
choicesEnglish: There's too much of a crowd at the entrance.
There's too much joy in the bar.
There's too much boredom in the street.
There's too much peace in the house.
correctAnswerEnglish: There's too much of a crowd at the entrance.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Difficoltà a entrare per la massa di persone.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica un assembramento eccessivo davanti ai cancelli.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Difficulty getting in because of the mass of people.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates an excessive gathering in front of the gates.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Celebrare il trionfo finale della propria squadra.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, parla del successo stagionale del club.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Celebrating your team's final triumph.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it speaks of the club's seasonal success.

choicesItalian: Il portiere ha parato il bacio.
Il tifoso ha gridato il nome.
Il tecnico ha perso il mixer.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Il gioco è concluso ufficialmente.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è arrivato il segnale di termine della partita.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! The game is officially over.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the match termination signal has arrived.

choicesEnglish: Have a good trip to us!
Enjoy your meal to all of you!

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un augurio collettivo prima del fischio d'inizio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai augurando piacere per lo spettacolo sportivo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A collective wish before the opening whistle.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are wishing pleasure for the sporting show.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Cercare l'accesso corretto allo stadio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai cercando il passaggio per entrare negli spalti.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Looking for the correct access to the stadium.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are looking for the passage to enter the stands.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Spiegare perché le informazioni sul biglietto sono sbagliate.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indichi un difetto grafico sul titolo d'ingresso.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Explaining why the information on the ticket is wrong.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you indicate a graphic flaw on the entry ticket.

english: Let's go to the stands right away.
choicesEnglish: Let's go to the stands right away.
Let's go to the bar later.
Let's go to the street now.
correctAnswerEnglish: Let's go to the stands right away.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un invito a prendere posto per non perdere l'inizio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, suggerisci di muoversi verso i sedili dello stadio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! An invitation to take your seat so as not to miss the start.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're suggesting moving toward the stadium seats.

choicesEnglish: I shouted too much on the street.
I laughed too much at the bar.
I paid too much for the tour.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Descrivere l'esultanza fisica dopo un punto segnato.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, parli della tua reazione entusiasta al goal.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Describing the physical exultation after a point scored.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you speak of your enthusiastic reaction to the goal.

choicesEnglish: Let's look for the black bag.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Uscire qualche minuto prima per non restare bloccati.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, proponi di stare lontani dall'assembramento all'uscita.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Leaving a few minutes early not to get stuck.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you propose staying away from the crowd at the exit.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Segnalare una truffa o un errore grave.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai dicendo che l'ingresso non era autentico.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Reporting a scam or a serious error.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are saying that the entry was not authentic.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Coordinate spaziali tipiche degli spalti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indichi la zona e la linea di sedili.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Typical spatial coordinates of the stands.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're indicating the area and row of seats.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Ricordare i successi passati della squadra.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, parli della vittoria ottenuta nella stagione precedente.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Remembering the team's past successes.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're talking about the victory obtained in the previous season.

english: This year we are doing poorly.
choicesEnglish: This year we are doing poorly.
This year we are doing well.
This year we were born today.
correctAnswerEnglish: This year we are doing poorly.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Lamentarsi della cattiva posizione in classifica attuale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica una situazione sportiva negativa.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Complaining about the current bad standing.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates a negative sports situation.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Esprimere piacere nel guardare lo spettacolo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa che ti stai divertendo molto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Expressing pleasure in watching the show.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means you're having a lot of fun.
